beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and prepare for the day
beats.reasoning: Jackson starts every day with intention; even on a transit strike day, he's up early and ready to move. Low energy yesterday (30/100) means he'll prioritize his swim ritual to reset.
Non-negotiable ritual; Jackson can't start the day without a swim. Only a 10-minute walk from home, so even with low energy yesterday, he'll make this happen. Sets him up mentally and physically.

beats.activity: Return home, dress, grab coffee and breakfast
beats.reasoning: Quick turnaround after swim; he'll need to eat and prep for work. Transit strike means he'll need to drive or rideshare to Interbay—better to leave with a full stomach.

beats.activity: Commute to work via personal car or rideshare
beats.reasoning: Transit strike in effect; rideshare surge pricing makes personal car likely. Northgate to Interbay is ~15–20 min under normal conditions; budget 35 min for potential traffic and strike-related congestion.

beats.activity: Work at school: morning counseling sessions with students
beats.reasoning: Standard morning work block; Jackson engages in one-on-one and small-group counseling. Mild, partly cloudy weather means he might have outdoor recess duties or nature-based check-ins with kids.
Jackson typically eats at his desk or in the break room. Good weather today (68F, partly cloudy) is a mild reprieve from yesterday's heat and smoke, so he might sit outside briefly to decompress.

beats.activity: Afternoon counseling sessions and administrative tasks
beats.reasoning: Post-lunch work block; end-of-day prep for students as the school day winds down. Thursday energy is building, and Jackson will likely have follow-up check-ins or planning for next week.

beats.activity: Commute home from work via personal car
beats.reasoning: Transit strike ends at 4pm; Jackson leaves work at 3:30pm, so he'll still drive or use rideshare. Interbay to Northgate ~15–20 min normally; budget 30 min for lingering traffic.

beats.activity: Run errand: pick up groceries and yarn at QFC
beats.reasoning: Jackson is a regular at this store; he's been eyeing yarn for projects and will grab fresh groceries. It's on the way home and fits his pattern of combining errands efficiently. Mild weather is perfect for a quick shop.
Short walk from QFC back to Northgate home. Jackson settles in, puts away items, and transitions to leisure time. Energy is recovering after yesterday's low of 30/100.
Jackson cooks a simple, nourishing meal—nothing fancy, consistent with his recent pattern. He values quiet time to decompress after a day of holding emotional space for students.

Evening wind-down ritual. Jackson works on his new merino-blend project while listening to a podcast. Mild evening weather (68F) means he might sit on the porch briefly, but mostly he stays indoors. This settles his nervous system before bed.
Jackson gets a solid 7.5 hours of sleep, ending well before 6am. Low energy yesterday means he'll want deep rest tonight to recharge for Friday.

diary:
Started the day right with my swim at Northgate Pool—nothing clears the head like those early morning laps. The water was cool and quiet, just how I like it. Drove to Interbay afterward; the transit strike had traffic a bit snarky, but I was grateful I had my car. The counseling sessions were heavy today—two kids dealing with some friendship stuff, one worried about his parents. These conversations matter, even when they wear me out.

Grabbed lunch in the staff room and powered through the afternoon paperwork. By the time I got home, I was ready to just be still. Hit QFC for groceries and some new yarn—found a lovely slate blue that's been calling to me. Made a simple dinner, then settled in with my knitting and a podcast about urban ecology. The needles clicking, the story unfolding... that's when I finally exhaled.
thought_process: The transit strike didn't really shake me—I've got my car and a flexible enough schedule. If anything, it made me appreciate not having to jostle with crowds today. The morning swim was non-negotiable; at 59, I know what keeps me sane. Work felt emotionally demanding but purposeful. Those kids needed someone to listen, and I was present for that. By afternoon I was running on fumes, so I made a conscious choice to keep the evening gentle: groceries, knitting, something low-key. I notice I'm craving texture and rhythm lately—the yarn, the podcast, the routine. Maybe it's the season shifting, or maybe it's just my pace these days. Either way, I honored it.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
